summary: OKX WebSocket supports all needed channels (candles 1m/5m/4h/1d, tickers, books, funding-rate, open-interest, mark-price, trades). REST supports history and snapshots. python-okx SDK exists (~850 stars) but is community-maintained. Rate limits: 60 req/2s for market data. WebSocket: 50 connections/IP, 240 channels/connection. Key limitation: no message buffer on reconnect.

Freqtrade (52k★) is the reference for signal generation — vectorized strategy evaluation, TA-Lib, ccxt exchange abstraction. Jesse (8.1k★) excels at no-lookahead backtesting and native multi-timeframe. Hummingbot (19k★) has the best connector system but is MM-focused. OctoBot (6.2k★) has basic LLM trading. No mature open-source project implements true multi-agent AI trading — this is a gap.

Key patterns: (1) Confidence-weighted voting beats deliberative consensus (83.43% accuracy). (2) Disagree-or-Commit (DoC) protocol structured critique before commitment. (3) CrewAI best for role-based financial agent orchestration. (4) DeepSeek-V4 has 1M context window, affordable at ~$12-39/month for 1000 calls/day. (5) Two-tier architecture: independent analysis → confidence-weighted aggregation → optional debate round.

Most robust crypto factors ranked: (1) Order Book Imbalance L1-L3 (Sharpe 2-4), (2) Taker Flow Imbalance (Sharpe 2-3), (3) Funding Rate Extremes + OI (Sharpe 1.5-2.5), (4) Short-term Reversal (Sharpe 1-2), (5) Volume Momentum (Sharpe 1-1.5). Crypto-specific: RSI 80/20 not 70/30, BB(20,3) not (20,2), EMAs need 1.5-2× faster parameters. Factor decay 3-5× faster than equities.
